title: Arguments & Parameters 🗣️
description: Pass data into your functions to make them flexible.

slides.title: Make It Flexible 🤸
Defining Parameters 🏗️
Passing Arguments ⚾
Multiple Parameters 💻
slides.content: You can have as many parameters as you want! Just separate them with commas.

quizData.question: What happens if a function is expecting 2 parameters, but you only give it 1 argument when calling it?

options.text: The missing parameter receives the value 'undefined'
The function refuses to run at all

quizData.explanation: JavaScript is extremely forgiving. If you miss an argument, the corresponding parameter inside the function is simply created as 'undefined'.

slides.title: Parameter Defaults 🛡️

quizData.question: What is the relationship between Parameters and Arguments?

options.text: They are exactly the same thing.
Parameters are defined in the function creation. Arguments are the real data passed when calling the function.
Arguments are defined in the function creation. Parameters are the real data passed when calling the function.
Parameters use numbers, arguments use strings.

quizData.explanation: Remember: P is for Placeholder (Parameter). A is for Actual data (Argument).
